claim: MaceOffExtractor subclasses BatchedFeatureExtractor to aggregate BDE-derived effective k_cat estimates from the existing cell_sim.layer1_atomic.engine.MACEBackend across an enzyme's substrate set. For each (enzyme, substrate) pair in the input DataFrame, the wrapper calls MACEBackend.estimate_kcat and aggregates per locus_tag into 7 summary features: mean / std / min / max k_cat, substrate count, mean confidence, and has_estimate. Heavy deps (mace-torch, torch, rdkit, ase, e3nn) load lazily through MACEBackend._ensure_loaded. The cache parquet is not populated by this session; the Colab notebook runs the wrapper on GPU and writes cell_sim/features/cache/mace_off_kcat.parquet.
source_detail: Session 14. Verifiable by reading the module + running 5 sandbox-safe tests. The wrapper does NOT re-implement the BDE / Eyring / Hammond math — that already lives in MACEBackend.estimate_kcat since a prior session. External weights: mace-off23_small from the mace-torch package (Batatia et al. 2023). Those weights are NOT registered as a memory_bank source; the extractor is transparent plumbing around the existing layer1_atomic interface.

context.commit_range: TBD — filled in once Session 14 push lands.

caveats: The parquet is empty until the Colab notebook runs.

Per-substrate detail is aggregated into per-enzyme summary stats so the output satisfies the FeatureRegistry locus_tag index contract. A follow-up session can publish a separate sidecar source (e.g. 'mace_off_per_substrate') if the joint detector stack needs it.

The Syn3A SBML does not carry SMILES strings; the notebook's bootstrap passes species IDs as placeholders and relies on MACEBackend.estimate_kcat to return the 'mace_bde_failed' source for un-parseable strings. Rows with no usable BDE are skipped in aggregation — mace_has_estimate ends up 0.0 for those genes. A future session can plug in a curated species-to-SMILES map from KEGG or ChEBI and cite it properly.
No MCC measurement is claimed by this fact.
mace-off23_small downloads ~100 MB of weights on first call (inside MACEBackend._ensure_loaded). This happens on the Colab GPU, not in the sandbox.
